chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The Mudabbar (one whose emancipation is conditional upon the death of the testator) is from the third

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/37)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/37)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمُدَبَّرِ قَالَ هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْوَصِيَّةِ يَرْجِعُ فِيمَا شَاءَ مِنْهَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazal Bin Shazan, both together from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Amaar who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about Al-Mudabbar (conditionally emancipated one). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He is at the status of the bequest. It can be retracted regarding whatever one so desires to, from it’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father and Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about al-Mudabbar. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is like will which one can change in whatever way one likes.’”
